title: Dental Care for Veterans Act
plain_summary: The Dental Care for Veterans Act would let all veterans get dental care through the VA, just like they can get other medical care. Right now, only some veterans can get dental care from the VA if they have certain conditions or meet special rules. The new law would change this slowly over four years, with different groups of veterans becoming able to get dental care at different times. Veterans who need care the most would be able to get it first, followed by other veterans based on their disability level and other factors.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Dental Care for Veterans Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Requirement That Secretary of Veterans Affairs Furnish Dental Care in the Same Manner as Any Other Medical Service
sections.text: This section changes how the Department of Veterans Affairs provides dental care to veterans. The law removes special restrictions on dental care and makes it available just like other medical services. The changes will happen gradually over four years. Veterans who already get dental care from the VA will keep getting it right away when this law starts. Other veterans will become eligible for dental care in groups over time. Veterans with the highest priority for VA health care will get dental care after one year. The next group will get it after two years. Another group will get it after three years. The last group will get it after four years. The law also updates other parts of the legal code to match these changes and removes outdated sections about dental care restrictions.
